data.title: SpringWorks gets EU endorsement for genetic disorder treatment
data.description:
SpringWorks Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SWTX) said that an advisory panel of the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Mirdametinib, as a treatment for patients with neurofibromatosis type 1, aged 2 years and older.

Issuing a so-called positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for the company's MEK inhibitor, Mirdametinib as a treatment for symptomatic, inoperable plexiform neurofibromas. This includes pediatric and adult patients with neurofibromatosis type 1, a genetic disorder.

The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization, which is expected in the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Frontline Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18, revenue of $428.09M
data.description:
• Frontline press release (NYSE:FRO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18.
  • Revenue of $428.09M (-31.1% Y/Y).

data.title: Wolfspeed appoints former executive vice president to newly created COO position
data.description:
Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) has appointed David Emerson, an industry veteran and former executive vice president with the company, to the newly created role of chief operating officer to help the silicon carbide technology company improve gross margins and return to profitability.

Shares climbed 5% during early market action on Friday.

On Tuesday, it was reported Wolfspeed was planning to file bankruptcy at some point in the next few weeks. As a result, shares plunged 59% by the close of Wednesday's trading session. They rebounded 34% on Thursday. Still, shares are down 74% year to date, and are trading at less than $2 per share, or about 1.4% of the all-time high of $137 set in November 2021.

Emerson, who formerly served as the executive vice president of Wolfspeed's LED Products division when the company was still called Cree, was hired to run the Operations, Supple Chain and Quality divisions.

"Having previously led our LED business through market disruption and global expansion, Dave brings a wealth of industry expertise and strategic insight which positions him well to drive operational excellence at Wolfspeed," said Wolfspeed CEO Robert Fuerle. 

Emerson is expected to play a critical role in improving manufacturing quality and efficiency, accelerating time-to-market, and ensuring consistent delivery.

"My experience driving operational excellence has equipped me with the skills to help develop best-in-class solutions for our blue-chip customers, all while working to accelerate the Company's path to profitability," Emerson said.

Seeking Alpha analysts point to Wolfspeed's shrinking gross margins as evidence of the company's severe struggles.

"Wolfspeed posted Q3 earnings on May 8, and it became even clearer to me that this company has no future," said Seeking Alpha analyst Josh Arnold. "Revenue fell from $201M to $185M year-over-year, despite Mohawk Valley Fab contributing $78M, up from $28M a year ago."

"The larger issue, in my view, is the fact that Wolfspeed's gross margins continued their freefall," he added. "Q3 of last year saw adjusted gross margin of 15% of revenue, but that fell to just 2% in this year's Q3. I don't recall any company I've ever seen post gross margins near zero. No business can survive that way."

Seeking Alpha analyst Henrik Alex said if Wolfspeed ends up filing a Chapter 11 bankruptcy, it's likely common equity holders would end up with nothing.

"With $6.65B in debt ranking ahead of the common equity, a recovery for shareholders in bankruptcy looks highly unlikely," Alex said. "For my part, I would expect unsecured creditors to emerge as the restructured company's new owners, with current equity holders likely being wiped out."

data.title: Aequus Pharmaceuticals announces sale of Zimed PF to Luvo Medical Technologies
data.description:
• Aequus Pharmaceuticals (OTCQB:AQSZF) and Luvo Medical Technologies announced on Friday the signing of an asset purchase agreement for the sale of Zimed PF.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Luvo will pay Aequus a lump sum payment of C$400,000 at the closing of the transaction and an ongoing royalty of 6% on gross profit attributable to the sale of Zimed PF until July 29th, 2029.
  • Until closing, Luvo will be responsible for managing distribution and commercialization efforts of Zimed PF. If existing inventory is not depleted by closing, Luvo will purchase the outstanding inventory.
  • The Transaction is expected to close in Q4 2025 upon the transfer of the Drug Identification Number.
  • Aequus expects to call a special meeting of shareholders to be held in Q3, 2025 for approval of the Transaction.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: AAPL, AMZN, BAH and CPRT
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Friday slumped, after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened tariffs against Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) and the European Union. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday: 

  • Shares of Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) were -3.6% in pre-market trading, after Trump threatened to slap a 25% tariff on the tech giant's products unless it built its iPhones at home. "I have long ago informed Tim Cook of Apple that I expect their iPhone’s that will be sold in the United States of America will be manufactured and built in the United States, not India, or anyplace else," Trump said on his Truth Social service. "If that is not the case, a Tariff of at least 25% must be paid by Apple to the U.S.," the President added.
  • Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) stock was -3.2% ahead of the opening bell. A regulatory filing on Thursday showed that shareholders had rejected a proposal that would require the e-commerce and tech firm to permanently separate the roles of the chief executive and board chair. Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) was also in focus the previous day after Bill Ackman's activist hedge fund Pershing Square disclosed that it had taken a new position in the Magnificent 7 member. Pershing sees AMZN's valuation as "attractive" and pointed to the strength of its dual business model. 
  • Shares of Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE:BAH) were -14.2% ahead of market open, after the McLean, Va.-based company issued fiscal full year 2026 guidance that missed expectations. For FQ4 2025, Booz Allen (NYSE:BAH) reported adjusted profit per share that came in-line with the consensus, while missing out on revenue estimates. BAH provides cyber and artificial intelligence technology solutions primarily to the government for defense, civil and national security projects. 
  • Copart (NASDAQ:CPRT) stock was -4.9% in pre-market trading, after delivering a FQ3 2025 revenue miss. Executives on the earnings conference call pointed to softness in Copart's (NASDAQ:CPRT) insurance business, along with flat unit sales in its U.S. segment. "We have observed softness in the heavy equipment auction space, due in part to widespread uncertainty regarding infrastructure spending and tariffs," CFO Leah Stearns said. Copart (CPRT) runs online car auctions.

data.title: Merck contends single dosing of Gardasil vaccine should be avoided
data.description:
Despite indications that a single dose of Merck's (NYSE:MRK) human papillomavirus vaccine Gardasil could be as effective as the current multi-dosing schedule, the drugmaker said that an alternative dosing regimen should be avoided -- for now.

The current regimen calls for two or three shots to obtain full protection.

"Merck maintains that until there are sufficient data to enable the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and European Commission to license an alternate dosing regimen for GARDASIL 9, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention recommendation should remain consistent with the approved product labeling," the company said in a statement.

"Merck sought guidance from the FDA regarding its proposed single dose trial designs to ensure they would serve as an acceptable basis for a future change to the approved dosing regimens in the label. During these discussions, the FDA was clear about the high standard of evidence that would be required for the licensure of single dose regimens."

A study published in The Lancet Regional Health - Europe in February found that girls who received one or two Gardasil doses had "comparable effectiveness against high-grade cervical lesions" versus those who received three doses. That study is available here: https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanepe/article/PIIS2666-7762(24)00347-8/fulltext.

data.title: Palantir sees bullish views at Wedbush after $795M US army contract modification
data.description:
Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and its $140 price target on Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) after the company was awarded a $795M U.S. Army modification to contract for Maven Smart System software licenses.

The firm said that Palantir is an increasingly significant player within the U.S. government.

"Palantir remains one of our top names to own in 2025 and this deal represents another opportunity for PLTR to capitalize on while continuing to generate unprecedented traction for its entire portfolio across in the federal and commercial landscapes," said Analysts led by Daniel Ives.

The contract, is the Department of Defense, or DOD's, AI initiative for data fusion and target identifications, where work locations and funding will be determined with each order with an estimated completion date of May 2029. This modification will be in addition to its prior nearly $480M Maven Smart System received in May 2024, bringing the total value of the contract up to around $1.3B representing Palantir's first ever billion-dollar-contract as the federal government continues expanding deals with Palantir, the analysts added.

Ives and his team believe this deal represents an additional tailwind for Palantir with AI initiatives across the U.S. government accelerating with AI a strategic focus on the federal front and "Palantir in essence in the sweet spot to benefit from a tidal wave" of federal spending on AI across North America and Europe following the recent NATO partnership.

In addition, the analysts said that earlier this week, President Trump announced the initial plans for a Golden Dome air and missile defense shield for the U.S. inspired by Israel’s Iron Dome. The ‘Golden Dome’, an ambitious project will be led by U.S. Space Force General Michael Guetlein, which as per Trump will cost about $175B over the next three years with the first roughly $25B already carved out in 2026’s U.S. defense budget if it passes through the House.

The analysts said that it is highly expected that Palantir will be one of the main providers and beneficiaries, as the stepped-up AI investments being seen under the Trump Administration have and will continue to benefit Palantir within the Beltway.

Ives and his team noted that Palantir's unique AI software approach will be a positive growth catalyst as governments are to increase efficiency with more software/lower headcount. Outside the massive Maven extension, Palantir was recently awarded a $30M contract by the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, or ICE, agency to build an ‘ImmigrationOS’ Surveillance Platform and received a $400M extension ($100 million in ARR) with U.S. military to extend access to its Maven Smart System AI tool to all branches.

The analysts continue to believe that Palantir will continue to gain more deals across the federal government with its software value proposition playing perfectly into the broader efficiency theme while these programs/contracts in the DOD are safe and not at risk of getting cut despite this new spending environment given the high priority.

Shares of Palantir fell about 2% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Modiv details plan related to reverse stock split, subsequent forward split
data.description:
Modiv Industrial (NYSE:MDV) said on Friday that the net-lease REIT was seeking shareholder approval for a reverse stock split to address the cost issue associated with dormant accounts, and the process will be followed with a forward split if implemented.

Shares were +1.34% pre-market to $14.40.

The fourth proposal in the company's proxy statement filed for the 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders seeks investor approval for a share combination of the common stock anywhere between 1:500 to 1:1,500 at any time prior to December 31, 2026.

"Given that the vast majority of these seemingly dormant accounts hold somewhere more than 500 but less than 1,500 shares, we identified that a large reverse split, held in place for only a brief moment in time before reverting the share price back to a more normal share price via a forward split, is the most effective way to address this legacy administrative burden and cost issue," said Modiv CEO Aaron Halfacre in a message to investors that aims to provide clarity regarding the rationale and impact of the reverse stock.

"The thinking is…implement a large reverse split, address the new batch of fractional shares, implement a forward split, and then back to business with reduced cost and less administrative burden – all in one fell swoop," added the CEO.

"In a nutshell, if the reverse split is approved (and we are ok if you don’t want it), then the final ending result would be a normal stock price, less dormant accounts at our transfer agent, and more money saved," clarified Halfacre.

The detailed statement can be found here.

data.title: Gilead succeeds in late-stage trial for breast cancer therapy Trodelvy as a first line option
data.description:
Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) announced Friday that its antibody-drug conjugate Trodelvy reached the main goal compared to chemotherapy in a Phase 3 trial designed to test it as a first-line option against a form of breast cancer.

Citing topline data from its Phase 3 ASCENT-03 trial, the Foster City, California-based pharma giant said its cancer blockbuster reached the study’s primary endpoint of progression-free survival, indicating a statistically significant and clinically meaningful effect.

The global study was designed to evaluate Trodelvy compared to the physician’s choice of therapy in about 540 newly diagnosed patients with locally advanced, inoperable, or metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (mTNBC).

Patients didn’t harbor cancer biomarker PD-L1 or were ineligible to receive immunotherapy.  

As for safety, the company said Trodelvy indicated a safety profile consistent with prior studies, and there were no new safety signals. Investigators are set to continue ASCENT-03 until data are available for overall survival, the trial’s secondary endpoint, which was not mature at the time of the interim readout.  

Trodelvy is currently approved in the U.S. as a late-line option for TNBC.

data.title: Bayer wins EU recommendation for label expansion of Regeneron-partnered Eylea
data.description:
Bayer A.G. (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY) said that an advisory panel of the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed the label expansion of a high-dose version of its ophthalmic drug, Eylea, with extended 6-month treatment interval.

Issuing a so-called positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the label expansion of an 8 mg dose of Eylea, developed in partnership with Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN).

The recommendation supports expanded treatment intervals of up to six months for two major retinal conditions: neovascular age-related macular degeneration and diabetic macular edema.

The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization, which is expected within the next few weeks.
